meta.title: Art Studio & Events | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: Design and make items, then practice presenting them at supportive showcases—confidence-first, pressure-free.

hero.title: Art Studio & Events
hero.subtitle: Create, share, and celebrate work in supportive settings—confidence and pride first.
hero.bestFor: Creators who light up when people see and appreciate what they make.

blocks.html: Art Studio & Events is about creating, sharing and entrepreneurship. Participants will build their personal brands by designing and making things like; digital art, photography, crafts, 3D prints and more. Then they practice presenting them at events or markets. It's about confidence, pride, and safe exposure to micro-enterprise concepts.

items: Simple products: stickers, prints, keychains, small crafts
Building sets of items for a future display table or online gallery
Learning how to set up a table or display
Practicing how to talk about their work
Participating in low-pressure showcases or markets (when available)
Basic ideas of pricing and value (age-appropriate and person-centered)
Understanding that their creativity has worth
When appropriate, exploring simple ways items could be gifted, raffled, or sold under family/agency guidance

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.prompt: Want to plan a first set of items or showcase goal?

blocks.html: Participants create art, prints, and small projects, then practice sharing them at events and showcases — building confidence, communication skills, and pride in their work.

Art Studio & Events uses Goods & Services-funded sessions to support the person in designing and creating items (digital or physical) and, where appropriate, participating in small showcases or events. Emphasis is on skill development, social interaction, and self-esteem rather than commercial sales.
